title: Gondara

'''Gondara''' was an [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ando/Legends|Ando]]. She was raised by her older brother, [[Karlon]], after their [[Parent/Legends|parents]] were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Karlon went on to join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and Gondara spent [[Time/Legends|time]] on an Alliance [[Safe world/Legends|safe world]] to avoid potential Imperial reprisals over her brother's actions. When Gondara was seventeen, Karlon was killed in [[Battle of Bresnia|battle]] on [[Bresnia]]. Karlon's best friend, the [[Carosite]] [[Medic/Legends|medic]] [[Tamo Lan]], took responsibility for the teenager, and she joined him in serving with [[Shroud Team]], a unit of [[Foster Agent]]s—tasked with assisting stranded operatives in returning to the Alliance—in [[Corint City]] on the planet [[Pirik]]. An able [[Thief/Legends|thief]], Gondara trained under Lam to be his medical assistant.

==Biography==
Gondara was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]]<ref name="Shroud" /> [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] between [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]] and [[13 BBY/Legends|13 BBY]]<ref name="G-age" /> on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ando/Legends|Ando]]. Her [[Parent/Legends|parents]] were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], leaving her to be raised by her elder brother, [[Karlon]]. Several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the death of their parents, Karlon joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], an insurgent movement dedicated to overthrowing the Empire. Gondara was sent to live on an Alliance [[Safe world/Legends|safe world]] so that she would be protected from any Imperial attempt to use her to get to Karlon while he served with the Alliance during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Shroud" />

Sometime after [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], when Gondara was seventeen, Karlon was killed in the line of duty [[Battle of Bresnia|fighting]] Imperial forces on the planet [[Bresnia]]; Gondara chose to enter active service with the Alliance following her brother's death. Karlon's best friend, the [[Carosite]] [[Medic/Legends|medic]] [[Tamo Lan]], took responsibility for Gondara, taking her under his wing as his charge. Lan was serving under the [[Jin]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Dutra Zeneta]] as part of [[Shroud Team]], a unit of [[Foster Agent]]s responsible for helping stranded operatives return to the Alliance, in [[Corint City]] on the planet [[Pirik]]. Despite not being a veteran [[Agent/Legends|agent]], Gondara was a skilled [[Thief/Legends|thief]] when she joined the unit. She served as a Foster Agent alongside the rest of the team, which also included the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Dheendo]] and the [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]] [[Thilis-Brin]].<ref name="Shroud" />

Gondara's assignment was one of the most dangerous roles in the entire Alliance; Foster Agents were constantly threatened with exposure from Imperial forces, as their names and locations were common knowledge to Alliance operatives in the field who may have need of their services. If an operative was to be captured and reveal the name of the Foster Agent under interrogation, the Agent was compromised.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref> Gondara also assisted Lan in his medical duties, and she began to develop the skills needed to operate as his assistant.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Personality and traits==
Gondara was an adolescent Aqualish training to be an effective member of the Alliance,<ref name="Shroud" /> although she was considered to be an adult of her [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref> Already a skilled thief and pickpocket by the [[Time/Legends|time]] she was assigned to Shroud Team, she also began to develop the necessary skills to serve as a medical assistant. She had some knowledge of other [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species and languages, and was well informed about the inner workings of Corint City itself. Capable of driving [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles, Gondara could also operate security equipment. She was able to defend herself from attack, and carried a [[Club/Legends|club]] for self-defense and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="Shroud" />

==Behind the scenes==
Gondara first appeared in "[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)|Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]," an article series that appeared in issues of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]''. Gondara was profiled in the article published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|tenth issue]], released in [[1996]], written by [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]]. An illustration of Gondara by [[Kathy Burdette]] accompanied her capsule.<ref name="Shroud" /> In [[2003]], Gondara was name-checked in [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' as an example name for Aqualish characters.<ref name="UAA" />
